LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER/PLAINTIFF YIBRAH TESFA'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY PEREMPTORY WRIT SHOULD NOT ISSUE IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MARCH 5, 2013 IN ORDER TO PERMIT CALPERS THE OPPORTUNITY TO FILE ITS OPPOSITION AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES PER LOCAL RULE 2.6B. ON DECEMBER 21, 2012, THE COURT ISSUED AN ALTERNATIVE WRIT. THE PARTIES WERE ORDERED TO FILE AND SERVE ANY OPPOSITION AND REPLY IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES DEPARTMENT 302 ON THE DAY OF FILING. THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS DOES NOT REFLECT THAT CALPERS FILED ANY OPPOSITION, AND DEPARTMENT 302 DID NOT RECEIVE ANY COURTESY COPIES. HOWEVER, ON JANUARY 31, 2013, PETITIONER FILED A REPLY THAT REFERS TO ARGUMENTS RAISED IN THE OPPOSITION. THUS, CALPERS SHALL FILE ITS OPPOSITION PAPERS THAT WERE PREVIOUSLY SERVED ON PETITIONER NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 6, 2013 AT 4:00 PM. CALPERS SHALL PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF ALL OPPOSING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, ON THE SAME DAY AS FILING. THE COURT HAS RECEIVED COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-26-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS ANNE STAUSBOLL, CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM AND MARCIA WIGINGTONYEE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF 42 U.S.C. 12132 AS AGAINST DEFENDANTS STAUSBOLL AND WIGINGTONYEE IN THEIR INDIVIDUAL CAPACITIES. THE DEMURRER IS ALSO SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION BROUGHT PURSUANT TO 42 U.S.C. 1983 AS AGAINST DEFENDANTS CALPERS, STAUSBOLL AND WIGINGTONYEE TO THE EXTENT THE CLAIMS WERE ASSERTED AGAINST THEM IN THEIR OFFICIAL CAPACITIES. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION BROUGHT UNDER 1983 AS TO DEFENDANTS STAUSBOLL AND WIGINGTONYEE IN THEIR INDIVIDUAL CAPACITIES. FINALLY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AS THERE IS NO BASIS FOR PLAINTIFF'S CONCERN THAT DEFENDANTS MAY IN THE FUTURE STOP PAYING HIS BENEFITS, AND PLAINTIFF IS NO LONGER A POSSIBLE CLASS REPRESENTATIVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR # 5963 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-18-2014 Continued To Feb-11-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Anne Stausboll, California Public Employees Retirement System, An Agency Of The State Of California, And Marcia Wigingtonyee'S Demurrer (Added To Calendar For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only).
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants Anne Stausboll, California Public Employees Retirement System, An Agency Of The State Of California, And Marcia Wigingtonyee'S Demurrer is a duplicate entry.

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID E. LYON, TEL. NO. 845-9324 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFFREY R. RIEGER OF REED SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 659-4883 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ANNE STAUSBOLL, CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM AND MARCIA WIGINGTONYEE'S DEMURRER
TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for violation of 42 U.S.C. 12132 as against Defendants Stausboll and Wigingtonyee in their individual capacities. (See Alsbrook v. City of Maumelle (1999) 184 F.3d 999, 1005 n.8 ["Title II provides disabled individuals redress for discrimination by a 'public entity.' See 42 U.S.C. 12132. That term, as it is defined within the statute, does not include individuals."].) The Demurrer is also sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action brought pursuant to 42 U.S.C. 1983 as against Defendants CalPERS, Stausboll and Wigingtonyee to the extent the claims were asserted against them in their official capacities. States and state officers sued in their official capacity are immune from liability for claims brought under 1983 pursuant to the 11th Amendment. (Catsouras v. Dept. of Cal. Highway Patrol (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 856, 892.) This ruling is not inconsistent with the Court's ruling on the first demurrer as the original Complaint named Defendant Stausboll only, and it was unclear whether she was being sued in her individual capacity, official capacity, or both. Thus, the determination of whether Defendant Stausboll was entitled to immunity was not apparent on the face of the Complaint. In contrast, here the Complaint clearly asserts the 1983 claims against all three Defendants, and against the two individuals in both their official and individual capacities. Thus, the SAC clearly discloses that sovereign immunity is a bar to recovery against the Defendants in their official capacities. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action brought under 1983 as to Defendants Stausboll and Wigingtonyee in their individual capacities. The allegations against Stausboll are mere conclusions and lack particularized facts demonstrating a constitutional deprivation. The Complaint fails to allege any wrongful conduct by Wigingtonyee. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all state law claims due to Plaintiff's failure to satisfy the claim presentation requirements of Gov. Code section 900 et seq. Plaintiff has made no showing or argument that any Defendants made any misrepresentations or concealed any material facts that Plaintiff relied on to decide not to file a claim. He points to nothing in the statements made by counsel during the writ proceedings that constituted an affirmative misrepresentation or concealment of material facts concerning the need for, or advisability of, a tort claim. Plaintiff's reliance on waiver, laches, equitable tolling, and continuing violations are all misplaced, and supported by no binding or persuasive authority. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for declaratory relief as there is no basis for Plaintiff's concern that Defendants may in the future stop paying his benefits, and Plaintiff is no longer a possible class representative. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR # 5963 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Continued To Nov-17-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2014 Continued To Dec-31-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar May-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey R. Rieger, Esq. of Reed Smith, Tel. 415.659.4883, for respondent and moving party;
David E. Lyon, Esq. of the Law Offices of David E. Lyon, Tel. 415.845.9324, for petitioner and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with modification. RESPONDENT ANNE STAUSBOLL's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is overruled on the grounds of res judicata and failure to obtain leave of court. Taking a petition off calendar and declining to rule on it because the relief sought has become moot does not constitute a judgment on the merits. Hollywood Circle, Inc. v. Dept. of Alcoholic Beverage Control (1961) 55 Cal.2d 728, 733. Moreover, Defendant cites no authority and the court finds none supporting a requirement that Plaintiff obtain leave of court prior to filing this action. Demurrer sustained as to the first through sixth causes of action with leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to allege with specificity the acts or omissions of Defendant that give rise to liability for each claim. Demurrer also sustained as to causes of action one through six on the ground that the Complaint fails to allege compliance with the claim presentation requirement. Gov. Code 810 et seq. Government Code section 915(b) is the governing provision, and it requires claims against the state to be presented to an office of the Victim Compensation and Government Claims Board. As to causes of action four through nine, Demurrer sustained with 20 days leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to allege with specificity the acts or omissions of Defendant that deprived Plaintiff of his constitutional rights. Demurrer overruled on the grounds that Defendant is immune from liability, as immunity is an affirmative defense. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Petitioner/Plaintiff Yibrah Tesfa'S Order To Show Cause Why Peremptory Writ Should Not Issue (Per Ex Parte Order Of 12/21/12)
Off Calendar Feb-05-2013 Continued To Mar-05-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner/Plaintiff Yibrah Tesfa'S Order To Show Cause Why Peremptory Writ Should Not Issue is continued on the court's own motion to March 5, 2013 in order to permit CalPERS the opportunity to file its opposition and provide courtesy copies per Local Rule 2.6B. On December 21, 2012, the court issued an alternative writ. The parties were ordered to file and serve any opposition and reply in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure and to provide courtesy copies Department 302 on the day of filing. The Register of Actions does not reflect that CalPERS filed any opposition, and Department 302 did not receive any courtesy copies. However, on January 31, 2013, Petitioner filed a reply that refers to arguments raised in the opposition. Thus, CalPERS shall file its opposition papers that were previously served on Petitioner no later than February 6, 2013 at 4:00 pm. CalPERS shall provide courtesy copies of all opposing papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, on the same day as filing. The court has received courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
